skill.name: ai-tools
skill.description: Reference for all AI tools available in DBX Studio's AI chat system. Use when adding, modifying, or debugging AI tool definitions, tool execution, or provider integrations.
skill.summary: Reference skill for DBX Studio AI chat tools including schema reading, query execution, and data visualization
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: All 42 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for DBX Studio AI tools. Detected patterns (Ruby backticks, Windows SAM, weak crypto) are markdown formatting, database references, and SQL function mentions in documentation - not executable code or security vulnerabilities.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected backtick syntax in markdown - false positive for Ruby/shell execution in documentation file
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation with code examples. No actual shell execution - markdown backticks for formatting detected as Ruby backticks by scanner.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Data Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected 'Windows SAM database' - false positive for sensitive data exposure
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: DBX Studio is a database IDE. References to database systems in documentation are expected, not sensitive data exposure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected weak crypto mentions - false positive for actual cryptographic vulnerabilities
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Database IDE documentation may reference SQL hashing functions (MD5, etc.). These are SQL function references, not cryptographic implementations in skill code.

content.user_title: Add or Debug AI Tools in DBX Studio
content.value_statement: DBX Studio users need a reference for AI chat tools to query databases. This skill provides definitions for schema reading, query execution, and data visualization tools.
actual_capabilities: Read database schema information using read_schema tool
Get table data with pagination using get_table_data tool
Execute SQL SELECT queries safely using execute_query tool
Generate charts from query results using generate_chart tool
Describe table structure using describe_table tool
Get table statistics using get_table_stats tool
limitations: Only SELECT/WITH queries allowed via execute_query tool for safety
Tool execution requires existing DBX Studio connection to database
Does not provide direct database connection management

use_cases.title: Database Developer Queries Data
use_cases.description: Developer uses AI chat in DBX Studio to ask questions about their database schema and run ad-hoc queries

use_cases.title: Data Analyst Visualizes Results
use_cases.description: Analyst generates charts from SQL query results directly in the AI chat interface

use_cases.title: AI Tool Developer Extends System
use_cases.description: Developer adds new AI tools or modifies existing tool definitions in DBX Studio

prompt_templates.title: List All Tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all tables in my database schema
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see available database tables

prompt_templates.title: Query with Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the top 10 customers by order value as a bar chart
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants SQL query results visualized

prompt_templates.title: Table Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Describe the structure of the users table
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants column details for a specific table

prompt_templates.title: Table Statistics
prompt_templates.prompt: Get row counts and index info for all tables

output_examples.input: Show me all tables in my database
output_examples.output: Your database has 12 tables: users, orders, products, customers, invoices, payments, inventory, suppliers, employees, departments, transactions, audit_log

output_examples.input: Generate a pie chart of orders by status
output_examples.output: Chart generated showing: Completed (45%), Pending (30%), Cancelled (15%), Returned (10%)

best_practices: Use quoted SQL identifiers like "schema"."table"."column" for cross-database compatibility
Leverage Drizzle ORM for database queries instead of raw SQL
Use tool descriptions to help AI understand when to apply each tool
anti_patterns: Do not allow non-SELECT queries through execute_query tool (security risk)
Avoid hardcoding database credentials in tool definitions
Do not skip schema validation when adding new tools

faq.question: What AI tools are available in DBX Studio?
faq.answer: DBX Studio provides read_schema, get_table_data, execute_query, generate_chart, describe_table, and get_table_stats tools for AI chat interactions.

faq.question: Can I execute INSERT or UPDATE queries through AI chat?
faq.answer: No. The execute_query tool only allows SELECT and WITH (CTE) queries for safety. Data modification requires direct database access.

faq.question: How do I add a new AI tool to DBX Studio?
faq.answer: Define new tools in apps/api/src/orpc/routers/ai/tools.ts following the existing tool pattern with name, description, and schema.

faq.question: What database providers does DBX Studio support?
faq.answer: DBX Studio uses Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL. The AI tools work with any PostgreSQL-compatible database.

faq.question: Can I generate charts from any query result?
faq.answer: Yes, the generate_chart tool can create bar, line, pie, and scatter charts from properly formatted query results.

faq.question: How does the AI know which tool to use?
faq.answer: Tool descriptions help Claude understand when to invoke each tool. The system prompt guides appropriate tool selection.
